meaning: adj.书信的,书信体的,用书信的
examples: In fact, a long epistolary scandal novel that she wrote in the mid-1680s sold quite well.
The Black Robe is an 1881 epistolary novel by famed English writer, Wilkie Collins.
All postal operators recognise, however, that the epistolary habit has taken a hit from the Internet.
As scriptwriter, Mr Yong partly retains the epistolary form of the novel and can turn a witty phrase.
Lauren Myracle, a regular on these types of lists, came in at number one with her epistolary Internet Girl books.
